question.text: A call option has strike price Rs 1,170 and current underlying futures is Rs 1,280. Option premium is Rs 200. What is the time value?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Intrinsic Value = 1,280 - 1,170 = Rs 110. Time Value = Premium - IV = 200 - 110 = Rs 90. **The Trap:** Rs 110 is intrinsic value, not time value. Rs 200 is total premium. Rs 0 would only apply if the option were deep ITM with no time value. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers premium decomposition calculations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Rs 90 of time value out of Rs 200 premium means 45% of the premium will decay by expiry — ITM option buyers pay for both intrinsic value (which stays) and time value (which erodes).
